'''Jax Pavan''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. His father, [[Lorn Pavan]], was once employed at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]. He was trained by [[Even Piell/Legends|Even Piell]], and during the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] wielded a blue [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Following the Purge, he went underground, living in [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant's]] underworld. However, he was hunted by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], forcing him to do whatever he could to survive.

==Biography==
===Early life===
[[Lorn Pavan|Jax's father]] worked at the Jedi Temple until the Jedi realized [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] potential in his two-year-old son. However, after agreeing to let the Jedi train Jax, Lorn was requested to leave the Temple, as Jedi trainees were seldom allowed contact with their parents. Lorn acquiesced, but missed his son terribly, and became extremely hostile towards the Jedi.

Shortly before the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]], Lorn and his partner [[I-5YQ]] were chased by [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], who sought a [[Holocube/Legends|holocube]] Lorn had obtained from [[Hath Monchar]] which detailed the planned blockade of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. Lorn shut I-Five down for his own safety and entrusted him to an [[Tuden Sal|acquaintance]] with instruction to take the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] to the Jedi Temple. The acquaintance, however, saw the value in the modified droid and wiped his memory. Lorn was ultimately killed by Maul, who would in turn be defeated by then-Jedi Padawan [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], when he cut the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Sai tok/Legends|in half]] during the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends#Duel in Theed|Duel in Theed]]. It would be [[20 BBY/Legends|over a decade]] before Maul returned to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]].

As Kenobi's Padawan, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] befriended Jax Pavan and confided in him. Anakin gave Jax a gift, a [[pyronium]] crystal. Jax was [[Knighting Ceremony/Legends|knighted]] three months before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] ended.

During the Clone Wars, I-Five found himself attached to a [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit/Legends|Republic Mobile Surgical Unit]], with [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Den Dhur]] and Padawan [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]]. He worked at reconstructing his memories, and finally recalled Lorn's final request to him—to watch over his son. I-Five returned to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to find Jax and fulfill this request.

===After the purge===
[[File:JaxPavan_SOS.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Jax in the Coruscant slums with [[Laranth Tarak]].]]

Jax survived [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] living as a [[Private investigator/Legends|private investigator]] in the [[Yaam Sector|Blackpit Slums]]. During this time he cut his dark brown hair, dyed it black, and permanently depilated his beard, in order to disguise himself so he wouldn't be recognized as a Jedi. He discarded his Jedi robes for a "nondescript vest of black bantha leather, threadbare gray trousers, and black boots, with an ankle-length, gunmetal-colored [[Greatcoat/Legends|greatcoat]] over it all." His choice of clothing was deliberately chosen to create an image of a down-on-his-luck [[spacer]]. 

He worked with the underground organization [[Whiplash]], who worked to create the "[[Underground Mag-Lev]]" to ferry politicians and others, including Jedi, who had incurred the Empire's wrath, off of Coruscant. Jax would eventually be reunited with his fellow freedom fighter [[Nick Rostu]], who told him that Jax's [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], [[Even Piell/Legends|Even Piell]], had before his death told Jax to find the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[10-4TO]], nicknamed "Bug-Eyes", who held information crucial to the burgeoning rebellion. Jax would also ally with [[Laranth Tarak]], [[I-5YQ]] and [[Den Dhur]]. During their search, Jax learned from [[Nick Rostu]] that [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] was seeking him, for a reason unbeknownst to anyone.

Jax and company tracked the droid, with the grudging help of [[Rokko]], to [[Factory District]] on Coruscant, where he found Bug-Eyes in the possession of [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]]. After a blistering [[Battle in the Factory District|battle]] with both the Falleen and the feral droids of the Factory District, Jax and company narrowly escaped the arrival of Darth Vader and returned to the sectors of Coruscant they called home. Although they could have left the planet, Jax was determined to stay and continue his work against the new order, and the others (the party now also included [[Kaird]] of the [[Nediji]] and [[Haninum Tyk Rhinann]]) agreed to stay with him.

===Whiplash===
[[File:Pavan_vs_Vader.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Pavan faces the fallen [[Chosen One/Legends|Chosen One]].]]
Jax and his group of friends became a part of the [[Whiplash]] movement on Coruscant, helping beings to escape offworld. One of their missions was to help the [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltron]] [[Dejah Duare]] leave the planet. Her partner, however, the famous [[Caamasi/Legends|Caamasi]] artist [[Ves Volette]], was murdered shortly after she came to them for help. They agreed to help her find Volette's killer. When Jax and his friends finally ended the investigation, they arranged for Dejah to leave, but were attacked at the spaceport by none other than the famed [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]], who Vader had hired to capture Jax. Sing engaged Jax in a [[Duel in Spaceport Nine|duel]], in which Jax survived by outsmarting Sing and dropping her into a reposicator, where he presumed she was killed. Afterwards, a gravely injured Laranth decided to leave Jax's group, while Dejah decided to stay on Coruscant and join it.

Several months later, Jax and Laranth [[Duel near Ploughtekal Market|found]] and decided to train a young Force-adept named Kajin Savaros, who was being hunted by the [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitorius]]. Kajin's anger issues made it difficult for Jax to train him, and eventually Dejah, whose romantic overtures had been repeatedly spurned by Jax, betrayed the group by allying herself with Inquisitor Probus Tesla and Darth Vader, leading to Laranth and Kajin being kidnapped. Vader erased Kajin's memories and sought to train him as one of the Inquisitors, and had Laranth tortured in an attempt to acquire the bota plant extract Barriss Offee had given to I-5 and the Sith holocron that Rhinann had stolen.

When Jax attempted a [[Battle in the Imperial Security Bureau|rescue mission]], Dejah revealed that she had betrayed the group because of a sensual addiction to the energies given off by Force-users, which Jax had been unwilling to allow her access to. Vader attempted to analyze the bota extract but it was absorbed by his system, causing an uncontrolled manifestation of Force energy that killed Dejah and injured Tesla, with Vader then attempting to kill Kajin and Jax. Rhinann attacked Vader and distracted him to the point where Kajin and Jax were freed, but paid for the effort with his life.

[[File:Pavan_and_Vader.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Confrontation in the Imperial Security Bureau.]]
Jax, Laranth, I-5 and Kajin escaped, aided by members of Whiplash, but Kajin's memories were possibly permanently erased, and Jax sent him to the planet of [[Shili/Legends|Shili]], where he could lead a peaceful life. Jax decided to stay on Coruscant and continue helping Whiplash, then confronted Laranth about their feelings for each other, refusing to allow her to dismiss the subject as she had previously done. Upon sharing their emotions through the Force for a second time, Jax and Laranth realized that they loved each other, however, they mutually came to a decision to not rush into anything.

When looking back at the previous battle, Jax remembered Vader demanding that he ''return'' the pyronium nugget. This led him to what he found to be an impossible conclusion, but dreaded it to be true: Anakin Skywalker, Jax's Padawan-time friend, was Darth Vader.

Returning to the group's dwelling, Jax experienced a momentary connection to the Cosmic Force, and realized that with the loss of the bota extract, only the Force itself could have made such an experience possible. Jax felt this was the Force's way of letting him know that he was fulfilling his purpose, and then turned his attention to a new client.

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Jax_Pavan.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Pavan during the Dark Times.]]
Jax was a deep thinker, often to the point of over thinking things. He would often wonder about the nature of a person's connection to the Force as well as himself and his decisions. Although he had had rather long brown hair and a beard and wore the typical robes of a Jedi before Order 66, Jax afterward dyed his hair black, permanently removed his beard, and took to wearing a long, high-collared, gray greatcoat.

==Powers and abilities==
Jax was skilled with the Force, but he lacked a constant connection with it. He viewed the Force as ribbons of color that extended into multiple dimensions, with which he could manipulate the things around him, view the "auras" of people, or sense webs of personal connections. He was also skilled with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and [[Lightwhip/Legends|lightwhip]]. He had been trained a bit with lightwhips at the Jedi Temple, but was not a master with that weapon. From his training under [[Even Piell/Legends|Even Piell]] Pavan had at least basic knowledge of all the [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] forms but was especially proficient in [[Form II/Legends|Makashi]].

==Equipment==
Unlike most Jedi, Jax had a small stash of personal items which he had kept after the Purge. These included a [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] of [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], a crystal from [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], and a reliquary containing a stone Skywalker had given him. Also unlike most Jedi, Pavan used a red lightsaber during a duel with Aurra that belonged to her and was sent to Pavan by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gregar Typho/Legends|Gregar Typho]].

==Behind the scenes==
Jax Pavan's physical appearance resembles ''[[Star Wars]]''{{'s}} creator, [[George Lucas]], during his youth.
